sources.name: AMD technical article
facts: The hackathon emphasizes systems/performance, models/training, applications/UX, and agentic workflows/autonomy.
Prize signals include a USD 20000 cash pool in the AMD article, plus Radeon GPU, AMD Developer Cloud credits, workshops, mentors, and finalist visibility.

sources.name: Lablab AMD Developer Hackathon page
facts: Extra Challenge: Ship It + Build in Public.
Submission fields include project title, short description, long description, tags, cover image, video, slide deck, GitHub repository, demo platform, and application URL.
The Lablab page lists USD 21500+ total prize pool, Track 1/2/3 prizes, Hugging Face special prize, and judging criteria: Application of Technology, Presentation, Business Value, and Originality.

sources.name: ROCm MI300X performance guides
facts: ROCm publishes MI300X system, workload, and vLLM inference guidance.
vLLM inference has a preconfigured environment for testing popular models on AMD Instinct MI300X GPUs.

sources.name: Cloudflare Quick Tunnels docs
facts: Quick Tunnels are intended for testing and development only.
Cloudflare recommends a remotely managed tunnel for production use.
Quick Tunnels have no SLA/uptime guarantee, a 200 in-flight request limit, and no SSE support.

sources.name: Hugging Face Spaces API endpoint docs
facts: Every Gradio Space is automatically available as an API endpoint.

rejected_tracks.reason: InfraAgent serves Qwen through vLLM and does not fine-tune model weights.
The product is an incident-triage workflow, not an image/audio/video multimodal application.

criteria_mapping.current_project_evidence: FastAPI polling API, five-node LangGraph, deterministic observability tools, runtime truth contract, vLLM/Qwen integration path, and AMD proof harness.
criteria_mapping.remaining_gap: Public run must show live_vllm from AMD MI300X.

criteria_mapping.current_project_evidence: HF Space surfaces status, scorecard, runtime proof, evidence timeline, root cause, runbook, and War Room Packet.
criteria_mapping.remaining_gap: Final video and slides must be refreshed after live AMD proof.

criteria_mapping.current_project_evidence: Business / Ownership Lens maps alert fatigue, tool sprawl, ownership, MTTR, and governance into each run.
criteria_mapping.remaining_gap: No blocker beyond honest live-runtime proof.

criteria_mapping.current_project_evidence: Replayable incident packet with stable evidence IDs, rejected alternatives, runtime truth, and audit seal instead of a generic incident chatbot.
criteria_mapping.remaining_gap: Competitive counterargument must stay focused on proof depth, not platform breadth.

required_submission_scope: Runtime truth visible in the run
prize_scope_signals: Sponsor feedback about AMD Developer Cloud, ROCm, vLLM, Qwen, and tunnel friction
judge_gap_statement: fallback demo verified, live AMD/Qwen proof pending
one_minute_judging_thesis: InfraAgent ReplayOps turns a noisy infrastructure alert into a judge-verifiable War Room Packet. A bounded LangGraph workflow gathers evidence, cites stable IDs, rejects weak hypotheses, routes ownership, produces a human-approved runbook, scores itself, and exposes runtime truth. AMD MI300X unlocks local Qwen/vLLM critique so judges can see whether the agent used live model proof or honest fallback. The result is not another dashboard or RAG answer: it is replayable incident evidence with an audit trail.
freshness_cycle: Re-open official AMD and Lablab pages before each final rehearsal.
Run scripts/amd_runtime_rehearsal.py on the AMD VM.
Run scripts/refresh_public_demo.py after the AMD-backed API is healthy.
Update README, SUBMISSION, HACKATHON_READINESS, STATE, and state.json only from observed proof.

risk_matrix.risk: ROCm AgentOps overlaps on agent trust, audit outputs, Qwen/vLLM, and operational workflow scoring.
risk_matrix.counterargument: InfraAgent should emphasize incident-replay evidence IDs, root-cause citations, and War Room Packet verification rather than a broad agent-ops command center.

risk_matrix.risk: Aegis-like crisis automation projects may claim autonomous execution and broad operational scope.
risk_matrix.counterargument: InfraAgent rejects unverified auto-remediation and stays credible by requiring human approval for risky actions.

risk_matrix.risk: ROCmPort/CUDA migration projects may score highly on AMD technical depth.
risk_matrix.counterargument: InfraAgent is Track 1 agentic workflow: AMD matters because local Qwen/vLLM critique is visible inside the incident triage loop.

risk_matrix.risk: A broader AIOps or SOC entrant may look more complete at first glance.
risk_matrix.counterargument: InfraAgent is narrower but proves every step through evidence, citations, runtime truth, and packet export.

risk_matrix.risk: ROCm tooling projects may show deeper low-level AMD optimization.
risk_matrix.counterargument: InfraAgent uses AMD as the runtime unlock for an end-to-end agentic workflow, not as a generic benchmark.

risk_matrix.risk: Projects with stable hosting may outlast a Quick Tunnel.
risk_matrix.counterargument: Use a named Cloudflare Tunnel for final judging or refresh Quick Tunnel immediately before judging.

manual_skill_decision.reason: The repository is small enough for direct rg/code inspection; no external skill discovery runtime is needed for the contest path.
selected_manual_skills: official docs and GitHub issue research
manual_skill_decision.socraticode: skip: shared codebase-intelligence layer is unnecessary for the current small repo.

exploration_rule: Use codebase search to navigate, but verify exact strings, errors, identifiers, and claims with known-string rg/search before changing or submitting.
